subject: History
chapter: Chapter: When People Rebel – 1857 and After

key_points: The Revolt of 1857 was a large-scale uprising against British colonial rule in India involving sepoys, rulers, peasants and common people
Political causes included British annexation policies such as the Doctrine of Lapse and the removal of Indian rulers
Economic causes included heavy taxation, destruction of traditional industries and exploitation of peasants and artisans
Military causes included discrimination against Indian sepoys, low pay and the introduction of greased cartridges
Religious and social fears arose because many Indians believed the British were trying to change their traditions and religion
Important leaders of the revolt included Bahadur Shah Zafar, Rani Lakshmibai, Nana Sahib, Kunwar Singh and Begum Hazrat Mahal
The revolt failed because of lack of coordination, weak leadership and superior British military power
After the revolt, the British government ended the rule of the East India Company and brought India under direct rule of the British Crown
examples: Mangal Pandey’s attack on British officers that increased tension among sepoys
Rani Lakshmibai organizing resistance in Jhansi
Nana Sahib leading rebel forces in Kanpur
Kunwar Singh leading rebellion in Bihar
formulas: Causes of Revolt → Political + Economic + Military + Religious factors
Spread of revolt → Meerut → Delhi → Kanpur → Lucknow → Jhansi
Aftermath → End of Company rule + Direct British Crown administration
exam_tips: Explain different causes of the Revolt of 1857 with examples
Mention major leaders and their regions in answers
Explain why the revolt failed despite widespread participation
Describe the administrative changes introduced after the revolt
